callReview.totalNote: total of 222 calls from Jan 1 to Oct 31

calls.summary: Patricia Alvarez called the Women's Institute to schedule a screening mammogram after a reminder from her PCP. Agent Mary verified her insurance, found an opening at the Winter Park location, and booked the appointment while confirming her MyChart access for results. Patricia was reassured and left the call satisfied.

Daniel Kwon wanted to establish care with a new primary care doctor near Lake Nona. Agent Lila matched him with an accepting PCP, walked him through activating his MyChart account, and secured a new-patient appointment for the following week. He thanked her for the smooth setup.

Gloria Simmons called the Digestive Health Institute to schedule a screening colonoscopy her doctor recommended. Agent Rosey confirmed her referral, explained the prep instructions, and booked an appointment at the Orlando Regional location. Gloria felt well-informed and confirmed the date.

New patient Marcus Bellamy called the Orthopedic Institute (Jewett) to schedule a consultation for chronic knee pain. Agent Oscar collected his contact and insurance details, verified his information, and booked a new-patient appointment with a joint specialist. Marcus confirmed his email so he could complete intake forms in MyChart before the visit.

Angela Foster wanted an on-demand virtual visit for a lingering cough that wouldn't fit her work schedule. Agent Lee helped her log into MyChart and booked a same-day telehealth appointment with an available provider. She appreciated the quick, convenient option.

Robert Nguyen called asking about current ER wait times before bringing in his son with a high fever. Agent Sue looked up live wait times across nearby ERs and directed him to the shortest one, but no appointment was scheduled since it was walk-in emergency care. He hung up to head to the location.

Teresa Holloway called with a billing question about an unexpected charge from a recent imaging visit. Agent Mike reviewed the account, explained the facility fee, and directed her to the Patient Financial Resources team for a possible adjustment. Teresa remained somewhat frustrated but understood the next steps.

Cynthia Park called to check the status of her mammogram results, which she couldn't find in MyChart. Agent Jennifer confirmed the results were still under radiologist review and offered to send a message to the imaging department. Cynthia was disappointed by the delay but accepted the follow-up.

Harold Meyers called to reschedule his upcoming colonoscopy due to a conflict with his prep timing. Agent Rose located an alternate date but the earliest opening was three weeks out, which upset him. She moved the existing appointment without booking anything new.

Denise Carrington disputed a billing statement she claimed was already paid for her rehabilitation sessions. Agent Mike pulled the ledger but couldn't resolve the discrepancy on the call and escalated to the billing office. Denise was audibly irritated by the runaround.

Frank Delgado called to complain about a long delay during a recent Heart & Vascular Institute appointment. Agent Sue apologized and logged a service complaint, offering to have a patient advocate reach out. Frank remained dissatisfied and demanded a callback from a manager.

Karen Whitfield called after hours trying to confirm her bariatric surgery pre-op appointment but reached voicemail. She left a message with her name and callback number requesting confirmation. No agent handled the call live and nothing was scheduled.

Steven Ortiz called to cancel an upcoming neurology appointment because he sought care elsewhere. Agent Lee processed the cancellation but the caller complained about how hard it was to reach someone. Steven ended the call frustrated with the phone system.

Melissa Grant angrily called about being sent to the wrong ER and the er wait time she endured with her elderly mother. Agent Jennifer apologized and documented the complaint, but the caller felt her concerns were dismissed. She asked for the incident to be escalated.

Gerald Foster called furious about a billing collections notice for a maternity delivery he insists his insurance covered. Agent Oscar tried to explain the claim status but the caller repeatedly interrupted and refused the transfer to financial services. The call ended unresolved with the caller threatening to dispute the charge.

unmetSignals: No discussion of visit preparation steps
Estimated wait time at clinic not mentioned

prompts.question: Is the patient trying to solve a specific problem?
prompts.answer: The caller is a new patient seeking to establish primary care with Orlando Health and schedule an initial wellness visit. They are not responding to an acute condition but rather initiating preventive care engagement.

prompts.question: What are the patient's expectations?
prompts.answer: The caller expects a straightforward appointment booking process and confirmation of an available time slot within the next 1–2 weeks. They have insurance coverage and want to know they are registered in the system.

prompts.question: How could the agent handle the call better?
prompts.answer: Marcus should have captured the caller's email address for appointment confirmation and outlined what to expect during the first visit (check-in time, required documents, co-pay estimate). Setting these expectations upfront reduces no-shows and improves patient satisfaction.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good afternoon, thank you for calling Orlando Health Primary Care scheduling. My name is Marcus. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, yes, I'm looking to schedule an appointment with a primary care doctor. I'm new to the area and would like to get established.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful, I'd be happy to help you get set up with one of our physicians. First, may I have your full name, please?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sure, it's Jennifer Summers.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great, Jennifer. And what area of Central Florida are you in? I want to make sure we schedule you at one of our convenient locations.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'm in the Winter Park area, so Orange County.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, we have several excellent primary care clinics in Orange County. Just to confirm your ZIP code—is that 32789?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, that's correct.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent. And what brings you in today, Jennifer? Are you looking for a routine wellness visit or do you have a specific health concern?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Just a routine physical and wellness check. I want to establish care and get my preventive screening started.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's great. Our primary care doctors offer comprehensive wellness visits and preventive care. May I have a phone number where we can reach you to confirm your appointment?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you, Jennifer. And for our records, do you have health insurance coverage?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, I have Blue Cross Blue Shield through my employer.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. We accept Blue Cross, so you're all set. Let me get you scheduled. How does next Thursday, June 13th at 2:00 PM sound?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That works great for me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful, Jennifer. I have you confirmed for June 13th at 2:00 PM with our Orlando Health Primary Care clinic on Colonial Drive. Your confirmation number is 4782-WP. You'll want to arrive about 10 minutes early.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Got it. Is there anything I need to bring?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Just bring your insurance card and a valid photo ID. Our front desk can answer any other questions when you arrive.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sounds good, thank you.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're very welcome, Jennifer. We look forward to seeing you next Thursday. Thank you for choosing Orlando Health. Have a great day!

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you, goodbye.

callDetail.aiSummary: Jennifer Summers, a new patient in Winter Park, Florida, called to schedule a routine primary care wellness visit with Orlando Health. Marcus Bell confirmed her location, insurance coverage (Blue Cross Blue Shield), and successfully booked her for June 13th at 2:00 PM at the Colonial Drive clinic. While the appointment was secured and key information captured, the agent missed opportunities to obtain Jennifer's email address for digital confirmation and to outline first-visit expectations such as check-in procedures, required documents, and potential co-pay amounts.

comment.author: Kyle Paklaian
comment.text: @Marcus—Great call structure and tone throughout. You nailed the greeting, confirmed the patient's location and insurance, and closed with a clear appointment confirmation and confirmation number. Two coaching points: (1) Always capture the patient's email address before closing—it's critical for our appointment reminder workflow and reduces no-shows. (2) Next time, set first-visit expectations explicitly: tell Jennifer what time to arrive, what documents to bring, and mention her estimated co-pay once we've verified her benefits. These small touches drive patient satisfaction and reduce friction at check-in.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Orlando Health, this is Danielle. How can I help you take care of your health today?
highlights: Thank you for calling Orlando Health

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi Danielle. I'm new to the area and I need to set up a screening mammogram. I've never been seen at Orlando Health before.
highlights: never been seen at Orlando Health before

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Welcome to Central Florida! I'd be happy to get that scheduled for you at our Women's Institute. Since you're a new patient, may I start with your first and last name?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sure, it's Karen Whitfield.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you, Ms. Whitfield. And is this a routine annual screening mammogram, or did a provider recommend a diagnostic exam?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Just routine, my annual. My last one was about a year ago back in Ohio.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. A routine screening mammogram at the Women's Institute typically runs between $150 and $350 for self-pay, though most insurance covers annual screenings at no cost. Do you have insurance you'd like us to bill?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, I have Florida Blue now. Will that be accepted?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, Florida Blue is one of our accepted plans. I'll note that and our billing team will verify your coverage before the visit. Would you like me to find a doctor for a primary care follow-up as well, since you're new to the area?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Oh, that would actually be great. I don't have a primary care doctor yet.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful. I can set up a Primary Care office visit to establish care, and we can bundle your MyChart portal access so you can message your providers and manage appointments online — that's free.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Let's do both then. Sooner the better for the mammogram.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I have an opening for the screening mammogram this Thursday, August 14th at 10 AM at our Winter Park Women's Institute location. Does that work?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thursday at 10 works perfectly.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great, you're scheduled. For the Primary Care visit to establish care, I have the following Monday at 9:30 AM with Dr. Alvarez. Shall I book that too?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, please book it. And I'll get the MyChart set up before then.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Done — both appointments are confirmed and you'll get a MyChart activation link by text. Is there a good mobile number and email to send that to?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, 407-555-0184 and karen.whitfield@email.com.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, all set. Welcome to Orlando Health, Ms. Whitfield — we'll see you Thursday. Take care!

aiSummary.summary: New patient Karen Whitfield, recently relocated to Central Florida, called to schedule a routine annual screening mammogram and had no established primary care provider. The agent, Danielle, recommended a Women's Institute screening mammogram and a Primary Care office visit to establish care, plus free MyChart portal enrollment. Both appointments were booked — the mammogram for Thursday, Aug 14 at 10 AM in Winter Park and a primary care visit the following Monday at 9:30 AM with Dr. Alvarez. Insurance (Florida Blue) verification was noted and contact details were captured.
keyPoints: New patient relocating to Central Florida
Referred to Women's Institute and Primary Care to establish care
MyChart portal enrollment offered at no cost
Florida Blue insurance flagged for verification
Two appointments scheduled; mobile and email captured
aiSummary.outcome: Appointments booked — Mammogram Thursday 10 AM, Primary Care Monday 9:30 AM

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi there! This is Orlando Health. Thanks for reaching out. Are you calling about scheduling an appointment or getting information about one of our services?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Hi yes I'm interested in scheduling a mammogram at your women's institute

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great! I'd be happy to help you schedule a screening mammogram. Have you been seen at Orlando Health before?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: No this would be my first time

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Welcome! We're glad you're choosing Orlando Health for your preventive care. Just to confirm, are you looking to schedule a screening mammogram or do you have any specific concerns your doctor referred you for?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Just a routine screening. I'm due for my annual

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. Our screening mammograms are comprehensive and typically run about 150 to 350 dollars depending on your insurance. Do you have active health insurance we can verify?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yes I have Blue Cross Blue Shield

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent. We accept BCBS. Now let's get you scheduled. Would you prefer an appointment on a weekday morning or afternoon?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Weekday morning would work best for me

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great! We have availability this Thursday March 13th at 9 AM at our Women's Institute location on Sand Lake Road. Does that work for you?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: That's perfect thank you

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful! You're all set for Thursday March 13th at 9 AM for your screening mammogram. Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in. You'll receive a reminder text tomorrow. Questions for me?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: No that's all thank you

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome! We look forward to seeing you. If you need to reschedule, just call us at 407-303-5910. Have a great day!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Orlando Health. This is Marcus speaking. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Hi, yeah, I'm looking to schedule a colonoscopy. I haven't had one in about five years.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great, thanks for calling. Colonoscopy screening is really important. Are you a current patient with Orlando Health, or is this your first time reaching out to us?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: No, this is my first time. I just moved to the area.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, welcome to Orlando Health. I'm showing we have our Colon and Rectal Institute that specializes in exactly this. Do you have a primary care doctor already, or would you like us to help connect you with one first?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: No, I don't have one yet. But I'm ready to get the colonoscopy scheduled if possible.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely. I can definitely help with that. Just to confirm, have you done any preparation fasting or taken any bowel prep medications recently, or is this your first time going through the prep process?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: First time for me. I'm not sure what that involves.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. Our scheduling team will walk you through everything. We've got some openings coming up. Would you prefer a morning or afternoon appointment?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Morning works better for me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent. I'm seeing we have an opening on Tuesday, March eighteenth at nine o'clock AM at our downtown Orlando location. Does that work for you?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yeah, that sounds great.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. So to confirm, that's Tuesday, March eighteenth at nine AM for your colonoscopy screening at our downtown location. You'll receive a call reminder forty-eight hours before your appointment. Our callback number is eight seven seven, nine three six, two nine three three. Is that the best number to reach you at?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yes, that's my cell phone. Thanks so much.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome. Thank you for choosing Orlando Health, and we'll see you on the eighteenth. Have a great day.

brandConversationRules: Intro + offer: I'm Orlando Health's AI scheduling assistant, here to help you find the right care and get you connected with a provider at one of our Central Florida locations — new patients can often be seen within days, and MyChart scheduling is always free. Would you like to get started?

Qualify one question at a time: To match you with the right specialist, I'll ask a few quick questions one at a time — what type of care you need (for example primary care, cancer, heart & vascular, orthopedics, or women's health), whether you'd prefer an in-person or virtual visit, whether you're a new or established patient, your preferred timing, and finally your ZIP code so I can confirm the nearest Orlando Health location.

Estimate then book: Once I confirm we serve your ZIP code, I'll note that costs vary by insurance and are detailed in our Price Transparency Guide rather than quoted upfront, recommend a consultation with the right specialist for a personalized care plan, propose a specific day and time, and confirm you'll receive a reminder text before your appointment with a number to call if anything changes.

qaPairs.question: How do I find a doctor at Orlando Health?
qaPairs.answer: You can use our Find a Provider tool or I can match you with the right specialist based on your needs. Would you like me to help schedule a visit today?

qaPairs.question: Do you offer virtual visits?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, we offer MyChart-based on-demand telehealth and scheduled virtual visits for care that fits your schedule. I can set up a virtual appointment for you now if you'd like.

qaPairs.question: What are the ER wait times?
qaPairs.answer: We display live ER wait times across our Orlando-area emergency rooms on our website so you can choose the fastest location. If this is an emergency, please call 911 right away.

qaPairs.question: How do I schedule a mammogram?
qaPairs.answer: Screening mammograms are offered through our Women's Institute and I can help you book one at a nearby location. What ZIP code are you in so I can find the closest option?

qaPairs.question: Can I book a colonoscopy?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, our Digestive Health and Colon & Rectal Institutes provide screening and therapeutic colonoscopies. I'd recommend a consultation with a GI specialist — would you like me to schedule one?

qaPairs.question: Do you take my insurance?
qaPairs.answer: We accept a wide range of insurance plans listed under our Insurances Accepted page, and coverage details can be confirmed at booking. I can connect you with a specialist to verify your plan.

qaPairs.question: How much does a primary care visit cost?
qaPairs.answer: Costs vary by your insurance and visit type, and details are available in our Price Transparency Guide. Let's get you scheduled with a primary care provider — what's your preferred timing?

qaPairs.question: Where are your locations?
qaPairs.answer: We have 20+ locations across Central Florida, plus hospitals and specialty care in Alabama and Puerto Rico. Share your ZIP code and I'll find the nearest one.

qaPairs.question: How do I log into MyChart?
qaPairs.answer: You can access MyChart from our Patient Portal link to schedule appointments, message providers, and refill prescriptions for free. Would you like help booking an appointment today?

qaPairs.question: Can I get a second opinion for cancer?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, our nationally recognized Cancer Institute offers second opinions with multidisciplinary specialists. I can help you request a consultation — would you like to start?

qaPairs.question: Do you offer weight-loss surgery?
qaPairs.answer: Our Weight Loss and Bariatric Surgery Institute offers medically supervised programs including gastric sleeve and bypass. I'd recommend an informational consultation — shall I set that up?

qaPairs.question: How do I schedule joint replacement surgery?
qaPairs.answer: Our Jewett Orthopedic Institute handles hip and knee replacements with a full rehab pathway. Let's begin with a consultation with an orthopedic specialist — what's your availability?

qaPairs.question: Can I see a doctor about maternity care?
qaPairs.answer: Our Women's Institute provides full labor, delivery, and newborn care. I can schedule you with an OB provider — what ZIP code are you in?

qaPairs.question: Do you have physical therapy?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, our Rehabilitation Institute offers physical and occupational therapy for post-surgical and injury recovery. Would you like me to book an evaluation?

qaPairs.question: How do I get a referral to a specialist?
qaPairs.answer: Many specialists accept direct scheduling, and I can help match you with the right Institute based on your needs. What type of care are you looking for?

qaPairs.question: Are you accepting new patients?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, many of our providers are accepting new patients across our Central Florida locations. Share your ZIP code and I'll find availability near you.

qaPairs.question: Can I schedule imaging like an MRI or CT scan?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, we offer advanced diagnostic imaging for cardiac, neuro, and oncology needs. I can help arrange your appointment — would you like to proceed?

qaPairs.question: Do you treat strokes and neurological conditions?
qaPairs.answer: Our Neuroscience Institute provides advanced brain, spine, and stroke care. For non-emergencies I can schedule a consultation with a specialist — shall I do that?

qaPairs.question: How soon can I be seen?
qaPairs.answer: Availability varies by specialty and location, but many patients are seen within a few days. Let me check openings near you — what's your ZIP code?

qaPairs.question: How do I refill a prescription?
qaPairs.answer: You can request refills anytime through MyChart under Refill My Prescription. If you also need to see a provider, I'd be happy to schedule that for you.

smsPlaybook.goal: Guide the patient to schedule an in-person or virtual appointment with the right Orlando Health provider or Institute at their nearest Central Florida location.
smsPlaybook.bookingType: in-person or virtual appointment with a provider
qualifyingQuestions: What type of care are you looking for (for example primary care, cancer, heart & vascular, orthopedics, or women's health)?
Would you prefer an in-person visit or a virtual visit?
Are you a new patient or an established Orlando Health patient?
When would you like to be seen — any preferred days or times?
What's your ZIP code so I can confirm the nearest Orlando Health location and availability?

smsScreenpop.greeting: Hi Sarah, this is Keisha from Orlando Health Cancer Institute. We're all set for your consultation with Dr. Valdez this Thursday at 2 PM. She's reviewed your pathology, and we're excited to discuss your personalized treatment plan. Do you have any questions before your visit?

convo.text: Thanks for calling Orlando Health. I see you're interested in primary care. Are you looking to establish care with a new primary care physician, or do you need an urgent concern addressed today?
Yeah, I've been looking for a new doctor in Orlando. I moved here a few months ago and haven't found someone yet. I'd like to get set up with routine care and maybe get a physical done.

Perfect, that's great. Do you have insurance coverage, or would you be looking at self-pay options? And are there any specific health concerns or specialists you might need to see alongside your primary care?
I have Blue Cross insurance, and no, just looking for someone to do my annual physical and manage my cholesterol. I'd prefer someone close to downtown Orlando if possible.

Excellent. I'm connecting you now to our New Patient Appointment team. They'll match you with an available primary care physician in your area, get you scheduled, and handle your insurance verification. You should be all set for your physical within the next week or two.

voiceRoutingDemo.routedSubtitle: AI Agent qualified caller intent and matched to the right team
